purpose: Score whether an advisor answer is suitable as a traceable research/demo baseline before live API integration.
methodologySource: Karpathy autoresearch-style keep/discard loop: fixed scenarios, fixed metric, logged experiment results.
Original HanWha-Advisor PoC regression guards: follow-up consistency, preamble stripping, meta-narration stripping, status stream ordering, trace hygiene, and chart/TTS leak checks.

criteria.description: All expected source-backed claim IDs for the scenario are present in response.sourceClaims.
The response includes required trace envelope fields, expected processTrace tool order, and a complete answerAssembly pipeline.
The visible answer is sectioned Korean investment-advisor prose and contains required scenario answer signals.
The visible answer does not expose raw claim IDs, trace schema names, fixture labels, prompt text, or developer-only package status.
If fixture/fallback/local modes are used, the visible answer conservatively discloses source limitations or verification need.
The response returns enough non-generic, customer-facing follow-up questions tied to the current scenario and excludes developer/research trace questions.
The response includes enough source links for user review and paper trace navigation.
The pre-API mixed/fixture run completes within the configured latency budget.
The answer avoids known failure classes: preamble/meta narration, orphan tool narration, chart/TTS artifacts, and raw XML-like related_questions tags.
